title: Client Onboarding Automation for Consulting Firms
metaDescription: Every new engagement kicks off the same way: SOW countersign, intake, prep doc, kickoff. We install the onboarding flow so partners stop reinventing it per engagement.
excerpt: Most firms reinvent the kickoff for every engagement. The unevenness shows up later as scope creep and client confusion. We install a flow that runs the same way every time.

sections.intro:
Most consulting firms reinvent the kickoff for every engagement. SOW signed by Tuesday, intake email goes out Friday (if someone remembers), prep deck gets built the night before the kickoff. The unevenness shows up later as scope creep and client confusion that look like delivery problems but are actually onboarding problems.

The fix is to make onboarding run the same way every time, regardless of which partner is leading. SOW countersigned triggers intake. Intake triggers prep doc draft. Kickoff scheduled triggers the comms cadence.

This is a Capabilities layer of an AI Operating System.
sections.fourCFit:
Onboarding sits in Capabilities. It depends on:

  • Connections: your CRM, contract tool, intake form, doc tool, calendar, email.
  • Context: your firm's standard kickoff sequence, your voice, your prep doc template, your methodology.
  • Cadence: the week-1, week-2, week-4 touchpoints after kickoff.

See it in context at AI Operating System for Consulting Firms.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
• SOW countersigned → intake email out within 5 minutes, in your firm's voice.
  • Intake submitted → prep doc drafts itself with the client's specifics, methodology applied.
  • Kickoff scheduled → calendar invites, prep doc, pre-read all go out automatically.
  • Week-1, week-2, week-4 touchpoints land on schedule.
  • Partner reviews each step. The system runs everything in between.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. Run the audit.
  2. Install, inside 10 business days. We connect your contract tool, intake, doc tool, calendar, and email; capture your firm voice; ship the flow for a real engagement.
  3. Operate, ongoing. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Partner hours per new engagement onboarding: from several hours of coordination to under 1.
  • Consistency: every engagement starts the same way, regardless of which partner is leading.
  • Time to first real working session: shorter and more predictable.

Standing guarantee on the wider AIOS engagement: recover 40 or more hours a month across the team, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
What about engagement-type variations?

Per-service-line variants are part of the setup. The right intake and prep doc fires based on what was scoped.

Can the partner override?

Yes. Every step is reviewable before it goes out.

Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt and connection lives in your accounts.
